enrichment.summary: Kyle recounts climbing Shiprock, a 1500-foot volcanic spire in New Mexico, when he was around 19-20 years old with a friend who was an experienced but reckless rock climber. He describes the illegal climb, the dangerous gear including a retired rope with a damaged sheath, carrying a six-pack of beer up the first unroped pitch, and a harrowing traverse on an overhang with a sheer drop to the ground. He reflects on how close he came to dying, mentions his climbing partner later died at 26 as the partner had predicted, and marvels at the recklessness of youth.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: the most gruesome death risk he ever took; wouldn't do it now at 44
incomprehensible in hindsight — young people don't grasp real danger
felt he was competent and almost invincible, but in retrospect was reckless and tempted fate
it came true; partner died at 26 as he said he would

entities: Katie (partner's girlfriend who later climbed with him)

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I've been thinking a lot lately about getting older and reflecting on the crazy things I used to do.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm glad I'm still alive after all the risky things I've done.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I smoke a lot of weed, which gives me time to think deeply about my past.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I experience PTSD-like symptoms when recalling some of my past adventures.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I climbed Ship Rock, a 1500-foot spire in New Mexico, when I was around 19 or 20 years old.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I climbed Ship Rock with a friend from Alaska who had only been rock climbing for a year.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I had some prior climbing experience, including a four-pitch climb with my brother and a guide in El Dorado.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Climbing Ship Rock was illegal, but we felt a sense of invincibility and special permission from the local Navajo people.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: We once climbed Cleopatra Spire after getting permission from local Indians to clean off wedding debris left by other climbers.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: During the Cleopatra Spire climb, I forgot a pocket knife to cut the wedding debris, causing my friend to descend and retrieve it.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The climb on Cleopatra Spire was terrifying because it was an 8.5-grade climb, which is extremely dangerous.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: We climbed Ship Rock's western side, planning a two-day trip, and had to navigate around overhangs and sheer walls.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: We used a retired climbing rope that was missing part of its outer layer, making it unsafe for serious climbing.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I carried a heavy backpack, including a six-pack of beer, while free-climbing a 60-foot sheer rock face without ropes.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I have a fear of heights, but I managed to climb Ship Rock despite it.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: We partied on top of Ship Rock after reaching the summit.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The descent was terrifying, especially traversing a 50-foot pitch with a sheer drop below.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: We almost reached the summit but turned back because my friend was unsure of the route and the rope was unreliable.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The climb took two full days, and I was exhausted driving back afterward.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I later learned that a climber died on Ship Rock after falling and tumbling down the rock face.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I also climbed Double Tower with the same friend, but had to do it barefoot because the shoes he gave me were too small.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: My friend later climbed Ship Rock with his inexperienced girlfriend, and she accidentally knocked a rock onto his head, nearly killing him.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: My friend died at 26, fulfilling his prediction that he would die young while climbing.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Looking back, I realize how reckless it was to climb Ship Rock without any possibility of rescue if something went wrong.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: When you're young, you take insane risks without considering the consequences, something I wouldn't do now at 44.
